wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the MoneyHey project overview section. Explain the personal finance management application's purpose, core features, and target audience. Document the technology stack including React 19.2.4, Supabase backend, and modern frontend architecture. Describe the application's key capabilities including transaction tracking, financial visualization, multi-wallet support, and reporting features. Include both conceptual overviews for beginners and technical highlights for experienced developers. Use terminology consistent with the codebase. Provide practical examples demonstrating common use cases and highlight the application's unique value propositions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for MoneyHey. Include detailed prerequisites covering Node.js version requirements, npm/pnpm package manager setup, and development environment configuration. Provide step-by-step installation instructions from repository cloning to dependency installation. Document the environment configuration process including .env file setup, Supabase project configuration, and AI provider integration (OpenAI/Gemini). Include initial project setup verification, development server startup commands, and basic usage examples. Address common setup issues and provide troubleshooting guidance for first-time users.

wiki_catalogs.name: Use Case Diagrams and System Overview
Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for MoneyHey's system design. Describe the layered architecture with presentation, business logic, and data access layers. Document the React-based frontend architecture, component hierarchy, and state management patterns. Explain the Supabase integration for authentication and database operations. Detail the service layer pattern implementation, repository pattern usage, and context provider architecture. Include system boundaries, component interactions, and data flow patterns. Address technical decisions, architectural trade-offs, and design constraints. Provide system context diagrams showing how MoneyHey integrates with external services like Supabase and AI providers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive authentication system documentation for MoneyHey. Detail the Supabase-based authentication implementation including login, registration, and session management. Document the AuthProvider context implementation, AuthContext state management, and protected route handling. Explain the authentication flow from user input through form validation to session persistence. Include security considerations, token management, and user session lifecycle. Provide code examples for authentication hooks usage, protected component implementation, and custom authentication logic. Address common authentication scenarios, error handling, and debugging techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Transaction Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed transaction management documentation for MoneyHey. Document the complete transaction lifecycle from creation through deletion, including validation rules, business logic, and data persistence. Explain the transaction service implementation, repository pattern usage, and domain model validation. Detail the transaction modal interface, filtering capabilities, and list display functionality. Include transaction categorization, amount processing, and date handling. Provide examples of transaction CRUD operations, bulk processing, and data import/export scenarios. Address transaction search functionality, pagination, and performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Wallet Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive wallet management documentation for MoneyHey. Detail the multi-wallet support system, wallet creation and configuration processes, and balance calculation algorithms. Document the wallet domain model, business rules for balance updates, and transaction-to-wallet associations. Explain wallet-specific analytics, currency handling, and balance synchronization across transactions. Include wallet management workflows, transfer operations, and wallet deletion procedures. Provide examples of wallet calculations, balance queries, and multi-wallet dashboards. Address wallet security considerations, data integrity, and performance optimization for large transaction histories.

wiki_catalogs.name: Dashboard and Analytics
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed dashboard and analytics documentation for MoneyHey. Document the dashboard layout architecture, financial summary card implementations, and interactive chart components. Explain the spending visualization system, quick actions functionality, and recent transactions display. Detail the dashboard service implementation, data aggregation algorithms, and real-time updates. Include chart configuration options, data filtering capabilities, and responsive design considerations. Provide examples of customizing dashboard widgets, adding new analytics components, and integrating additional financial metrics. Address performance optimization for large datasets and real-time data updates.

wiki_catalogs.name: Reporting and Data Visualization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive reporting and data visualization documentation for MoneyHey. Detail the expense analysis system, chart types and configurations, and data filtering mechanisms. Document the pie chart, bar chart, and line chart implementations with their specific use cases and customization options. Explain the transaction intelligence service for automated insights and pattern recognition. Include report generation workflows, data export capabilities, and visualization performance optimization. Provide examples of creating custom reports, configuring chart parameters, and implementing advanced filtering scenarios. Address data privacy considerations and report sharing functionality.

wiki_catalogs.name: UI Components and Styling
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed UI components and styling documentation for MoneyHey. Document the component library architecture, reusable UI elements, and responsive design implementation. Detail the theme system, Bootstrap integration, and CSS customization options. Explain component composition patterns, prop interfaces, and styling best practices. Include header navigation, sidebar functionality, and footer components with their configuration options. Provide examples of component customization, theme modifications, and responsive breakpoint handling. Address accessibility compliance, cross-browser compatibility, and performance optimization for styling.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Models and Domain Logic
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data models and domain logic documentation for MoneyHey. Detail the core domain entities including Transaction and Wallet models with their properties, relationships, and constraints. Document business rules implementation, validation logic, and data transformation processes. Explain the transaction mapping system, rule engines, and calculation algorithms. Include domain model diagrams, data validation rules, and business constraint enforcement. Provide examples of extending domain models, adding new business rules, and implementing complex financial calculations. Address data consistency, transaction atomicity, and domain-driven design principles.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Services and Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for MoneyHey's service layer and external integrations. Document the service architecture pattern, repository implementations, and external API integrations. Detail the authentication service, profile management, category services, and transaction intelligence API. Explain the Supabase client configuration, real-time features, and database integration patterns. Include service method signatures, parameter specifications, and response schemas. Provide examples of service usage, error handling strategies, and integration with external AI providers (OpenAI, Gemini). Address API versioning, rate limiting, and performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Schema and Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive database schema and configuration documentation for MoneyHey. Detail the Supabase database structure, table relationships, and data modeling decisions. Document the authentication tables, transaction schemas, wallet structures, and category hierarchies. Explain the repository pattern implementation for database operations, query optimization strategies, and data access patterns. Include database migration procedures, schema versioning, and backup strategies. Provide examples of complex queries, data relationships, and performance tuning. Address database security, access control, and data privacy considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: State Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed state management documentation for MoneyHey. Document the React Context API implementation, custom hooks usage, and global state synchronization patterns. Explain the authentication state management, user session handling, and protected route protection mechanisms. Detail state update flows, context provider architecture, and component state subscriptions. Include state persistence strategies, memory management, and performance optimization techniques. Provide examples of custom hook implementation, state composition patterns, and debugging state-related issues. Address concurrent state updates, error boundaries, and state reset procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Build and Deployment
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive build and deployment documentation for MoneyHey. Detail the Vite build configuration, development workflow, and production optimization settings. Document the package.json scripts, dependency management, and build pipeline processes. Explain the ESLint configuration, code quality checks, and pre-commit validation. Include deployment strategies for various platforms, environment variable management, and CI/CD pipeline setup. Provide examples of build customization, asset optimization, and performance monitoring. Address build troubleshooting, dependency conflicts, and deployment best practices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Troubleshooting and FAQ
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive troubleshooting and FAQ documentation for MoneyHey. Address common issues including authentication problems, transaction processing errors, and UI rendering issues. Document debugging techniques, error message interpretation, and solution workflows. Include performance troubleshooting for slow loading times, memory leaks, and chart rendering issues. Provide solutions for environment configuration problems, dependency conflicts, and build failures. Address user experience issues, accessibility problems, and cross-browser compatibility concerns. Include frequently asked questions about features, limitations, and best practices for optimal usage.

wiki_items.title: Use Case Diagrams and System Overview

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# MoneyHey - Personal Finance Management Application

## Project Introduction

### Purpose Statement
MoneyHey is a modern personal finance management application designed to help users track their income and expenses, monitor spending patterns, and gain insights into their financial health. Built with React and Supabase, it provides a comprehensive solution for personal budgeting and financial reporting.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Financial Tracking**: Enable users to record and categorize income and expenses
- **Real-time Analytics**: Provide interactive charts and reports for spending visualization
- **User Authentication**: Secure login system with session management
- **Responsive Design**: Cross-platform accessibility with mobile-friendly interface
- **Data Visualization**: Interactive charts for spending patterns and trends

### Target Audience
- Individuals seeking personal finance management solutions
- Users looking for a Vietnamese-language financial tracking application
- People interested in visualizing their spending habits through interactive charts

## Technical Architecture

### Component Breakdown

The application follows a layered architecture pattern with clear separation between presentation, business logic, and data access layers:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[React Frontend] --> B[Authentication Layer]
    A --> C[Presentation Layer]
    A --> D[Domain Layer]
    
    B --> E[Supabase Auth Service]
    C --> F[UI Components]
    C --> G[Pages & Routes]
    D --> H[Business Rules]
    D --> I[Validation Logic]
    
    E --> J[(Supabase Database)]
    F --> K[Bootstrap CSS]
    G --> L[React Router]
    H --> M[Transaction Rules]
    I --> N[Data Validation]
```

### Design Patterns

**1. Repository Pattern**: Data access is abstracted through repository classes (`transactionRepo.js`, `categoryRepo.js`) that handle all database operations.

**2. Service Layer Pattern**: Business logic is encapsulated in service classes (`transactionService.js`, `authService.js`) that coordinate between repositories and domain logic.

**3. Context Provider Pattern**: Authentication state is managed globally using React Context (`AuthProvider.jsx`) for seamless user session handling across components.

**4. Domain-Driven Design**: Clear separation between domain models (`transaction.js`, `wallet.js`) and application services.

### System Relationships

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant User
    participant App
    participant AuthProvider
    participant AuthService
    participant Supabase
    participant DB
    
    User->>App: Login Request
    App->>AuthProvider: Check Session
    AuthProvider->>AuthService: getUser()
    AuthService->>Supabase: auth.getUser()
    Supabase->>DB: Verify Session
    DB-->>Supabase: Session Data
    Supabase-->>AuthService: User Info
    AuthService-->>AuthProvider: Auth Result
    AuthProvider-->>App: Update State
    App-->>User: Dashboard Access
```

### Data Flow

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[User Input] --> B[Form Validation]
    B --> C[Transaction Service]
    C --> D[Transaction Repository]
    D --> E[Supabase API]
    E --> F[(PostgreSQL Database)]
    
    F --> G[Data Retrieval]
    G --> H[Transaction Mapper]
    H --> I[UI Components]
    I --> J[Charts & Reports]
```

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points
- **Application Bootstrap**: `src/main.jsx` - Configures React root, routing, and authentication provider
- **Routing Configuration**: `src/App.jsx` - Defines protected routes and navigation structure
- **Build Configuration**: `vite.config.js` - Vite build setup with React plugin

### Core Modules
- **Authentication System**: `src/context/AuthProvider.jsx` - Global authentication state management
- **Data Services**: `src/services/` - Business logic layer for all domain operations
- **API Layer**: `src/api/` - Direct database interaction with Supabase
- **Domain Models**: `src/domain/` - Business rules and validation logic
- **Theme System**: `src/theme/theme.js` - Color palette and styling constants

### Configuration Approach
- **Environment Variables**: Supabase credentials configured in `src/config/supabase.js`
- **Build Tools**: Vite configuration for development and production builds
- **CSS Framework**: Bootstrap integration for responsive design
- **State Management**: Custom hooks (`useAuth.js`) for component state

### External Dependencies
- **React 19.2.4**: Core frontend framework
- **React Router DOM**: Client-side routing
- **@supabase/supabase-js**: Backend-as-a-service for authentication and database
- **@nivo/charts**: Advanced data visualization components
- **Bootstrap 5.3.8**: Responsive CSS framework

### Integration Points
- **Supabase Authentication**: Email/password authentication with session persistence
- **Supabase Realtime**: Database operations with automatic UI updates
- **Material Symbols**: Iconography for consistent UI elements
- **Local Storage**: Client-side session and theme preference storage

### Component Relationship Diagram

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[App.jsx] --> B[AuthProvider.jsx]
    A --> C[ProtectedRoute.jsx]
    B --> D[AuthContext.js]
    B --> E[authService.js]
    E --> F[Supabase Auth]
    
    G[DashboardPage.jsx] --> H[Header.jsx]
    G --> I[Sidebar.jsx]
    G --> J[SummaryCard.jsx]
    G --> K[SpendingChart.jsx]
    G --> L[RecentTransactions.jsx]
    
    M[TransactionPage.jsx] --> N[TransactionList.jsx]
    M --> O[AddTransactionModal.jsx]
    M --> P[TransactionFilter.jsx]
    
    Q[ReportPage.jsx] --> R[ExpensePieChart.jsx]
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**1. User Authentication**
- Secure login/logout functionality
- Session persistence with automatic re-authentication
- Protected route system for authenticated pages only

**2. Financial Tracking**
- Income and expense categorization
- Multi-wallet support for different account types
- Real-time balance calculations and updates

**3. Data Visualization**
- Interactive pie charts for expense distribution
- Line charts for spending trends over time
- Bar charts for category-wise comparisons

**4. Transaction Management**
- CRUD operations for financial transactions
- Advanced filtering by date range, category, and wallet
- Bulk operations and transaction history

### Implementation Highlights

**Authentication Flow**: `src/context/AuthProvider.jsx` - Implements secure session management with automatic token refresh and user profile synchronization.

**Transaction Processing**: `src/services/transactionService.js` - Handles complex validation rules and ensures data integrity before database operations.

**Data Visualization**: `src/components/report/ExpensePieChart.jsx` - Utilizes @nivo library for interactive, responsive chart components.

**Responsive Design**: `src/theme/theme.js` - Comprehensive color palette and typography system for consistent user experience across devices.

### Feature Architecture Diagram

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Unauthenticated
    Unauthenticated --> Login: User enters credentials
    Login --> Authenticated: Successful authentication
    Login --> Unauthenticated: Authentication failed
    
    Authenticated --> Dashboard: Navigate to home
    Authenticated --> Transactions: Manage transactions
    Authenticated --> Reports: View analytics
    Authenticated --> Settings: Configure preferences
    
    Dashboard --> Authenticated: Navigation
    Transactions --> Authenticated: Navigation
    Reports --> Authenticated: Navigation
    Settings --> Authenticated: Navigation
    
    Authenticated --> Logout: User action
    Logout --> Unauthenticated: Session cleared
```

**Sources:**
- [package.json](package.json)
- [main.jsx](src/main.jsx)
- [App.jsx](src/App.jsx)
- [supabase.js](src/config/supabase.js)
- [AuthProvider.jsx](src/context/AuthProvider.jsx)
- [authService.js](src/services/authService.js)
- [transaction.js](src/domain/transaction.js)
- [transactionService.js](src/services/transactionService.js)
- [transactionRepo.js](src/api/transactionRepo.js)
- [DashboardPage.jsx](src/pages/DashboardPage.jsx)
- [Header.jsx](src/components/common/Header.jsx)
- [theme.js](src/theme/theme.js)
- [vite.config.js](vite.config.js)

</blog>
